source: Extreme Multi-label Learning for Semantic Matching in Product Search

concepts.finding: Formulates product search as XMC: queries -> relevant products (labels). Tree-based XR-Linear (PECOS) model achieves 60.9% Recall@100 with 1.25ms latency vs DSSM's 36.2% with 3.13ms latency. Hierarchical linear models with n-gram TF-IDF features enable logarithmic inference time. Outperforms BERT-based encoders when latency is constrained.
concepts.implication: Keyword-intent classification can be formulated as XMC: keywords = instances, intents = labels. Tree-based partitioning of the intent space enables logarithmic-time inference for large intent taxonomies. Sparse linear models (TF-IDF features) with hierarchical partitioning can match or exceed deep embedding models.
concepts.open_question: Is the keyword-intent mapping better modeled as a flat XMC problem or a hierarchical classification?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Tree-based XMC (XR-Linear) on TF-IDF keyword features achieves competitive accuracy to deep embedding methods for keyword-to-intent assignment, with order-of-magnitude lower latency.
